prose: **Pyridine Fukui reactivity (electrophilicity)** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, exact four-center integrals) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: single-point on the user-supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz` (no optimization). Partial charges: Mulliken (PySCF). SCF converged for neutral, cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2), and anion (charge -1, multiplicity 2) states; integrity status ok, trustworthy true; no warnings reported. The Mulliken Fukui plus index (f+) is largest at the nitrogen atom (atom 1) with f+ = 0.48422572, indicating the most electrophilic site. By contrast, the Mulliken Fukui minus index (f-) is largest at carbon 4 (atom 4) with f- = 0.1182626, indicating the most nucleophilic site. Command: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --charge 0 --mult 1 --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpb58fmr1h/live.json`.
